title: 🔴LIVE - What's Next for Archon - Live Roadmap Session
summary: Cole Medin hosted a live session discussing the roadmap and future direction of Archon. The session covered prioritized features and development plans for the open-source project available on GitHub.
writeup: A live session planning the Archon roadmap and designing a community workflow marketplace, with the author using Claude Code's ask-user-question tool to make design decisions in real time. Archon is an open-source AI coding harness builder that defines workflows as YAML, mixing deterministic script nodes with non-deterministic agent nodes to enforce step-by-step processes across multiple codebases. The session articulates a core architectural insight: embedding agents into a system-level harness prevents the step-skipping and non-determinism that occur when the entire workflow logic lives inside a single long agent session. The roadmap and a phase-one marketplace at archon.diy/workflows are the two outputs being planned.
key_points: Archon enforces AI coding workflows by running a harness above the coding agent: each node is either a deterministic script action or a bounded agent session, so the system controls sequencing rather than relying on the agent's reasoning across a long, error-prone context window.

The distinction between Archon and skills or IDE commands (such as those in Antigravity) is the inversion of control: skills build the system into the agent, while Archon builds the agents into the system, reducing the compounding risk of step-omission as workflow complexity grows.

The planned community marketplace (archon.diy/workflows) will let contributors submit YAML workflow files via pull request to a JSON registry, enabling any user to pull in a shared workflow with a single command, modeled loosely on the Claude Code plugin system.
Default workflows already ship with the Archon open-source install, giving new users concrete starting templates to run immediately and modify or use as prompts for their own coding agent to extend.

Archon and Hermes are complementary rather than competing: Hermes acts as a personal knowledge agent, and an Archon workflow can be invoked from within a Hermes session when a structured multi-step coding task needs to be executed.
The roadmap page design settled on a single-column vertical timeline with GitHub-style milestone cards, inspired by the node-graph layout of roadmap.sh but with cleaner visual styling.
